preamble: The shift from the alveolar nasal [n] to the high-back rounded vowel [u] is a form of vocalization. It typically occurs when an intervocalic or final nasal weakens into a labial glide [w], which then merges with the preceding vowel to form a nasalized diphthong or a pure vowel quality.

examples.note: Historical vocalization of the nasal into a labial glide/vowel, creating a nasal diphthong.
